items.title: It Takes Two rapper Rob Base, who helped bring hip-hop to the mainstream, dead at 59
items.summary_auto: Rob Base, rapper and one half of the Harlem hip-hop duo Rob Base & DJ E-Z Rock, has died after a battle with cancer at age 59.

items.title: The Pizza Huts from your childhood are making a comeback. Here's why nostalgia sells
items.summary_auto: A Pizza Hut franchisee in the U.S. is betting big on nostalgia, bringing back its '90s decor, Pac-Man and salad bars.

items.title: Your guide to the inaugural 'Steroid Olympics'
items.summary_auto: Two decorated Canadian athletes are among those competing at this weekend's inaugural Enhanced Games — which is either the future of sport or its downfall, depending on who you ask.

items.title: Trump calls a climate projection 'WRONG!' Turns out, climate action is working
items.summary_auto: Surely you've heard of RCP 8.5? Maybe not. But it's been in the spotlight since U.S. President Donald Trump who stirred up a conversation about a projection of future climate change that's rarely mentioned by name.

items.title: Kevin O'Leary's Utah AI plan shows the high cost of the data centre boom
items.summary_auto: Celebrity investor Kevin O'Leary wants to bankroll an AI data centre in rural Utah he says would be nearly identical to his proposed $70-billion, 7.5-gigawatt AI data centre campus in northern Alberta. But the Utah project has already been met with fierce opposition from some in the community who are worried about the potential environmental impacts.

items.title: As Gaza endures a ceasefire in name only, Trump's Board of Peace unable to make inroads
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump’s plan to rebuild Gaza is under growing strain as violence persists — more than 800 Palestinians have been killed since the ceasefire began — and his Board of Peace blames Hamas’s refusal to disarm for stalling progress.

items.title: Trump's Board of Peace struggles to progress beyond far-from-perfect ceasefire in Gaza
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump’s plan to rebuild Gaza is under growing strain as violence persists — more than 800 Palestinians have been killed since the ceasefire began — and his Board of Peace blames Hamas’s refusal to disarm for stalling progress.

items.title: Kevin O'Leary wants to build a massive AI data centre in Utah. Some residents aren't happy
items.summary_auto: Celebrity investor Kevin O'Leary wants to bankroll an AI data centre in rural Utah he says would be nearly identical to his proposed $70-billion, 7.5-gigawatt AI data centre campus in northern Alberta. But the Utah project has already been met with fierce opposition from some in the community who are worried about the potential environmental impacts.

items.title: Death toll from gas explosion at coal mine in China jumps to 90
items.summary_auto: Chinese President Xi Jinping called for ​authorities to "spare no effort" in treating the injured and conducting search-and-rescue ‌operations.

items.title: Laughing, barking, waiting 30 minutes to answer: Manitoba NDP's conduct at committees questioned
items.summary_auto: The Manitoba government's behaviour at legislative committee meetings is under criticism after the premier laughed and made barking noises at the Tory leader and the justice minister took 30 minutes to respond to a question from an Independent MLA.

items.title: 40,000 people under evacuation orders in California amid chemical tank leak
items.summary_auto: Authorities in Southern California on Friday were racing to figure out how to prevent the explosion of a storage tank that has been leaking a hazardous chemical used to make plastic parts, as some 40,000 people were under evacuation orders in the area.

items.title: Woman presumed drowned in Kamloops, B.C., river identified as Kenyan immigrant
items.summary_auto: A friend has identified the 24-year-old woman who is presumed drowned in a Kamloops, B.C., river after her car entered it last Sunday. Benina Jepkoech, who was originally from Kenya, arrived in B.C. last year.

items.title: Explosion at Staten Island dry dock leaves 1 dead, 36 injured
items.summary_auto: An explosion at a dry dock in New York City's Staten Island killed one person and injured 36 others on ‌Friday, as the blast occurred while firefighters were on the scene responding to a fire and attempting to ​rescue two people who were trapped, ​officials said.

items.title: Trump administration tells green card applicants to apply from abroad
items.summary_auto: Foreigners in the U.S. who want a  green card will need to leave and apply in their home country, the  Trump administration announced Friday, in a surprise change to a longstanding policy that sowed confusion and concern among aid groups, immigration lawyers and immigrants.

items.title: U.S. man loses appeal of $2.35M fine for starting wildfire in B.C.
items.summary_auto: A U.S. man whom investigators found likely started a wildfire in a sensitive ecological reserve in B.C. 's Sea-to-Sky region in 2018 has lost an appeal of a $2.35 million fine.

items.title: Colbert's farewell played it safe with warm, star-studded goodbye and no political bite
items.summary_auto: After CBS announced The Late Show's cancellation last year, Stephen Colbert warned viewers that "the gloves are off" — freeing the comedian to get political without consequence. But what unfolded on Thursday night seemed carefully calculated to comfort rather than confront.

items.title: Yves Sakila's death is being called Ireland's 'George Floyd moment'
items.summary_auto: Yves Sakila, a 35-year-old Congolese-born man, died last Friday after he was restrained outside a department store in Dublin, Ireland, by security guards.

items.title: Edmonton dog owner found guilty in fatal dog attack that killed 11-year-old boy
items.summary_auto: An Edmonton judge has found a dog owner guilty of criminal negligence causing death after a fatal dog attack killed an 11-year-old boy in 2024.

items.title: 911 audio reveals NASCAR's Kyle Busch was short of breath, coughing up blood day before death
items.summary_auto: NASCAR driver Kyle Busch experienced shortness of breath, felt he was overheating and was coughing up blood the day before his death, according to a 911 call obtained Friday by The Associated Press.

items.title: Job action averted as B.C. nurses reach tentative deal with province
items.summary_auto: British Columbia's nurses won't be taking job action, after reaching a tentative deal on a new collective agreement on Friday. It comes after members of the B.C. Nurses' Union voted 98.2 per cent in favour to give leadership a strike mandate earlier this month amid stalled contract talks. The union's last agreement expired in March 2025.

items.title: Ontario Premier Doug Ford slams 'ridiculous' court ruling that bars clearing an encampment
Remains of missing B.C. actor Stewart McLean found, homicide investigation continues
items.summary_auto: Stewart McLean was last seen at his residence in Lions Bay on May 15.

items.title: New protected marine reserve, larger than P.E.I., to be established on B.C.'s Central Coast
items.summary_auto: The reserve is within the Great Bear Sea, a diverse marine ecosystem that covers more than half of B.C. 's coast and includes glass sponge reefs, salmon, killer whales and migrating humpbacks.

items.title: Thousands protest Raúl Castro indictment outside U.S. Embassy in Havana
items.summary_auto: Thousands of Cubans gathered on Friday before the U.S. embassy in Havana to protest a U.S. decision to indict ‌former president Raúl Castro over the downing of two civilian airplanes 30 years ago.

items.title: Tulsi Gabbard steps down as U.S. intel chief, citing husband's cancer diagnosis
items.summary_auto: Tulsi Gabbard resigned as U.S. President Donald Trump's director of national intelligence on Friday, saying she needed to step away as her husband battles cancer. She is the fourth cabinet official to depart during Trump's second term, all of them women.

items.title: Ebola survivor calls for compassion as fear, unrest spread during outbreak
items.summary_auto: Dr. Kent Brantly became critically ill with Ebola while on medical mission work during the 2014 outbreak. The U.S. physician survived, and is now watching with concern at the speed and spread of the current outbreak — which has no approved treatments or vaccines available.

items.title: 3 dead after boat capsizes off Savary Island in B.C.: RCMP
items.summary_auto: Powell River RCMP say three men are dead and two have survived after a boat capsized about 500 to 1,000 metres off the shore of Savary Island Thursday night.

items.title: Hundreds of Jan. 6 defendants prepare to seek compensation from Trump’s Anti-Weaponization Fund
items.summary_auto: Hundreds of people prosecuted for crimes related to the Jan. 6 assault on the U.S. Capitol in 2021 plan to seek compensation from the U.S. government once a new taxpayer-funded anti-weaponization program is up and running.

items.title: Putin vows revenge after Ukraine attack kills at least 6, wounds dozens at student dorm
items.summary_auto: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Friday ordered his military ‌to prepare options to retaliate against Ukraine after he accused Kyiv of carrying out a deadly drone attack on a student dorm that he said killed six and wounded dozens of young people, with 15 still unaccounted for.
